evidence_quotes.quote: include online video games as well.
If games die, this would mean that the games at hand wouldn't be accessible to the consumer.
unfair to the consumer who put money into the game and is unable to use the product which they payed for.

paragraphs.text: I'm from the country of Malta, and I would like to ask that the Digital Fairness Act can also extend its influence to include online video games as well. There are many online games which are dead, the number being 358 games, or either at risk of dying, around 389 games. If games die, this would mean that the games at hand wouldn't be accessible to the consumer. This would not only be bad for media preservation, but also unfair to the consumer who put money into the game and is unable to use the product which they payed for.
